task_completion.description: Open the flight_analysis.ork rocket model in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket application is open, and the window title or design tree indicates 'flight_analysis' or 'clustered_motors' is loaded.

task_completion.description: Run the flight simulations to update their status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Flight simulations' tab of OpenRocket, at least 4 of the 5 simulation configurations show a green checkmark indicating they are up-to-date (no longer showing a red warning icon).

task_completion.description: Export time-series flight data to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows at least 3 CSV files located in the '/home/ga/Documents/exports/flight_data/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create the flight analysis report text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'flight_analysis_report.txt' located in '/home/ga/Documents/exports/'.

task_completion.description: The report compares motor configurations and recommends an optimal choice for a 250m target altitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text within 'flight_analysis_report.txt' explicitly mentions the '250m' (or 250 meters) target altitude, compares different motor configurations, and provides a clear recommendation based on the simulation results.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to run simulations and export data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with OpenRocket's 'Flight simulations' tab and 'Export data' dialogs, rather than bypassing the GUI.

integrity.description: The report contains genuine analysis rather than a copy-pasted prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report includes specific details like motor names, simulated altitudes, or flight metrics, proving the agent actually read the simulation results instead of just repeating the task description.

integrity.description: The exported CSV files are genuine OpenRocket exports.
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV files are seen being exported via OpenRocket's export dialog, or if their contents are visible, they contain comma-separated flight data headers (e.g., Time, Altitude, Velocity) rather than being empty files created via terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The rocket model contains exactly 5 simulation configurations which start as 'outdated'. To succeed, the agent must run at least 4 of these simulations (visible as green checkmarks in OpenRocket's Flight simulations tab). The agent must export at least 3 CSV files to the '/home/ga/Documents/exports/flight_data/' directory. The final report at '/home/ga/Documents/exports/flight_analysis_report.txt' must explicitly evaluate the motors against the 250m target altitude.
